title: Principles relating to processing
text: Personal data shall be: (a) processed lawfully, fairly, and transparently; (b) collected for specified, explicit, and legitimate purposes; (c) adequate, relevant, and limited to what is necessary (data minimisation); (d) accurate and kept up to date; (e) kept in a form which permits identification no longer than necessary (storage limitation); (f) processed in a manner that ensures appropriate security.

title: Lawful bases
text: Processing shall be lawful only if and to the extent that at least one applies: (a) consent; (b) contract necessity; (c) legal obligation; (d) vital interests; (e) public task; (f) legitimate interests (balanced against the data subject's rights).

title: Conditions for consent
text: Where processing is based on consent, the controller shall be able to demonstrate that the data subject has consented. Consent must be: freely given, specific, informed, unambiguous, AND as easy to withdraw as to give.

title: Special categories
text: Processing of personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, political opinions, religious or philosophical beliefs, trade union membership, genetic data, biometric data, health data, sex life, or sexual orientation shall be prohibited UNLESS one of the 10 exceptions in Art. 9(2) applies (e.g. explicit consent, vital interests, public health).

title: Transparent communication
text: Controller shall provide information in Arts. 13-14 + responses to Arts. 15-22 requests in a concise, transparent, intelligible, easily accessible form, in writing or electronically. Within ONE MONTH of receipt, extendable by 2 further months for complex or numerous requests with notice.

title: Right of access (DSAR)
text: Data subject has the right to obtain from the controller confirmation as to whether personal data are processed AND access to that data + (a) processing purposes; (b) categories of personal data; (c) recipients; (d) retention period; (e) right to rectify/erase/restrict/object; (f) right to lodge complaint with supervisory authority; (g) source; (h) automated decision-making + logic + significance + consequences.

title: Right to rectification
text: Data subject has the right to obtain rectification of inaccurate personal data + completion of incomplete personal data.

title: Right to erasure (right to be forgotten)
text: Data subject has the right to obtain erasure WITHOUT undue delay when: (a) data no longer necessary; (b) consent withdrawn + no other basis; (c) successful objection under Art. 21; (d) unlawful processing; (e) legal obligation; (f) child consent issue. NOT available where processing is necessary for: freedom of expression, legal obligation, public interest, public health, archiving/research/statistics, legal claims.

title: Right to data portability
text: Where processing is based on consent OR contract AND carried out by automated means, data subject has the right to receive personal data in a structured, commonly used, machine-readable format AND to transmit it to another controller without hindrance.

title: Right to object
text: Data subject has the right to object on grounds relating to their particular situation, to processing based on Art. 6(1)(e) (public task) or 6(1)(f) (legitimate interests). Controller shall no longer process unless compelling legitimate grounds override the rights of the data subject.

title: Records of processing
text: Each controller shall maintain a record of processing activities under its responsibility containing: name + contact of controller, purposes of processing, categories of data subjects + data, recipients, transfers (incl. country + safeguards), retention periods, technical and organisational security measures.

title: Security of processing
text: Controller and processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including: pseudonymisation + encryption; confidentiality / integrity / availability / resilience of systems; restore-ability; regular testing.

title: Breach notification to authority
text: Controller shall notify a personal data breach to the supervisory authority within 72 hours of becoming aware, unless the breach is unlikely to result in a risk to natural persons. Notification shall describe: nature of breach + categories + approximate number of data subjects + records; DPO contact; likely consequences; mitigation.

title: Breach notification to subjects
text: When the breach is likely to result in a HIGH risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the breach to the data subject without undue delay.

title: DPIA (Data Protection Impact Assessment)
text: Where processing is likely to result in a high risk, the controller shall carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data, especially for: systematic profiling; large-scale special-category data; large-scale public-area monitoring; biometric uniquely-identifying; new tech with high risk.

title: General principle for transfers
text: Any transfer of personal data to a third country or international organisation shall take place only if the conditions in Chapter V are complied with by the controller and processor.

title: Transfers subject to appropriate safeguards
text: Transfers may be made if appropriate safeguards are in place: (a) Standard Contractual Clauses; (b) Binding Corporate Rules; (c) approved code of conduct; (d) approved certification.

title: Administrative fines
text: Up to €10M or 2% of total worldwide annual turnover (preceding fiscal year) — whichever is higher — for infringements of: records of processing (Art. 30), breach notification (Arts. 33-34), DPIA (Art. 35), certification (Art. 42). Up to €20M or 4% for: basic processing principles (Art. 5), lawful basis (Art. 6), consent (Art. 7), special categories (Art. 9), data subject rights (Arts. 12-22), international transfers (Arts. 44-49).

title: Right to know / right of access
text: California consumer has the right to request that a business disclose: (a) categories of personal information collected; (b) sources; (c) business or commercial purpose; (d) categories of third parties shared with; (e) specific pieces of personal information collected about them.

title: Right to delete
text: California consumer has the right to request deletion of personal information. Business must comply unless an exception applies (completing the transaction, security, debugging, free speech, public-interest research, legal compliance, internal uses reasonably aligned with consumer expectations).

title: Right to opt-out of sale / sharing
text: California consumer has the right to opt out of the sale or sharing of personal information. Business must provide a clear and conspicuous 'Do Not Sell or Share My Personal Information' link. CPRA extended to include 'sharing' for cross-context behavioural advertising.
